title: Short title
summary: Establishes the short title of the chapter.

title: Definitions
summary: Defines key terms used throughout the chapter.

title: Creation of common-interest communities
summary: Outlines how to legally create a common-interest community.

title: Organization of unit-owners’ association
summary: Outlines the requirement for the organization of a unit-owners’ association.

title: Powers of unit-owners’ association; limitations
summary: Describes the powers and limitations of the unit-owners’ association.

title: Fiduciary duties of board members
summary: Defines fiduciary responsibilities and business judgment rules.

title: Election of board members
summary: Covers eligibility, disclosure, elections, and board certification procedures.

title: Liens against units for assessments
summary: Explains the rights of the association to place liens on units for unpaid assessments.

title: Maintenance of association records
summary: Mandates recordkeeping responsibilities and homeowner access rights.

title: Retaliatory action prohibited
summary: Prohibits retaliation by associations against homeowners who exercise their rights.

title: Injunctions
summary: Provides for court-ordered injunctions to stop violations of this chapter.
Outlines applicability and exceptions for protections related to homebuyers in common-interest communities.
Defines liability for the preparation and delivery of public offering statements to buyers.
Establishes general provisions for public offering statements, including required disclosures.
Details offering statement requirements for communities subject to developmental rights.
Grants purchasers the right to cancel a contract within a specified timeframe after receiving disclosures.
Explains the impact of violations on legal rights and damages for noncompliance with laws or documents.
Creates the Commission for Common-Interest Communities and defines appointment procedures.
Establishes the Ombudsman for Common-Interest Communities and outlines powers and responsibilities.
Defines what constitutes a violation under the chapter.
Outlines procedures for investigating violations and escalating complaints to the Commission.
Details possible remedial actions for violations, including fines and removal from office.
Authorizes the use of injunctions to stop violations of NRS 116 provisions.
